protocol.name: xfce_foreign_toplevel_management_private_v1
protocol.copyright:

interface.name: xfce_foreign_toplevel_manager_private_v1
interface.description:
This protocol advertises extensions to the existing wlr-foreign-toplevel-management protocol.
It is intended to be private to the Xfce desktop implementation, and should not be used by other applications. This protocol may introduce incompatible changes at any time, without warning, and without following normal, accepted guidelines about Wayland protocol versioning.

request.name: get_xfce_foreign_toplevel_handle
request.description: This creates a new xfce_foreign_toplevel_handle_v1 instance for the given zwlr_foreign_toplevel_handle_v1 object.

enum.name: state
enum.description: This enumeration includes toplevel states that are not a part of the base xdg-toplevel interface.

entry.name: shaded
entry.summary: The toplevel is 'rolled up' or 'shaded', usually meaning the window's title bar is visible, but the contents are not

entry.name: skip_pager
entry.summary: The toplevel should not be shown in the desktop pager view

entry.name: skip_tasklist
entry.summary: The toplevel should not be shown in a desktop task or window list

entry.name: sticky
entry.summary: The toplevel should be shown on all workspaces within its workspace group

entry.name: above
entry.summary: The toplevel should be stacked above other windows

entry.name: below
entry.summary: The toplevel should be stacked below other windows

request.name: move_to_output

description.summary: Moves the toplevel to another output

request.description:
This has no effect if the toplevel is already on the specified output.
The compositor may choose to ignore this request.

event.name: state

description.summary: Emitted when the toplevel's state changes

event.description: After all property changes have been made on this toplevel, the zwlr_foreign_toplevel_handle_v1's done event will be sent. Clients will only receive a single done event per set of property changes; that is, the done event related to events sent on the zwlr_foreign_toplevel_handle_v1 instance will be "delayed" to encompass events emitted on this interface as well.

arg.name: state
arg.summary: List of active states from the state enum

event.name: icon_name

description.summary: Emitted when the toplevel's icon name has changed

event.description:
If a themed icon name is set on this toplevel, this event will be emitted to inform clients of the toplevel's icon.
This event requires the xdg-toplevel-icon protocol, or a similar protocol that allows applications to associate icon names with their toplevels.
After all property changes have been made on this toplevel, the zwlr_foreign_toplevel_handle_v1's done event will be sent. Clients will only receive a single done event per set of property changes; that is, the done event related to events sent on the zwlr_foreign_toplevel_handle_v1 instance will be "delayed" to encompass events emitted on this interface as well.

event.name: icon

description.summary: Emitted when the toplevel's icon pixel data has changed

event.description:
If raw icon pixel data is set on this toplevel, this event will be emitted for each size/scale combination of icon data known to the compositor.
The pixel data will be sent in RGBA format, with 8 bits per channel (4 bytes per pixel).
The provided file descriptor can be mapped, with a size determined by multiplying the stride and height.
Implementations that do not support different renderings at different UI scale factors will always send 1 for the scale argument.
This event requires the xdg-toplevel-icon protocol, or a similar protocol that allows applications to associate icon pixel data with their toplevels.
After all property changes have been made on this toplevel, the zwlr_foreign_toplevel_handle_v1's done event will be sent. Clients will only receive a single done event per set of property changes; that is, the done event related to events sent on the zwlr_foreign_toplevel_handle_v1 instance will be "delayed" to encompass events emitted on this interface as well.

arg.name: icon_data
arg.summary: A file descriptor that can be mapped into memory

arg.name: scale
arg.summary: The UI scale factor this pixel data corresponds to

arg.name: width
arg.summary: Width of the icon, in pixels

arg.name: height
arg.summary: Height of the icon, in pixels

arg.name: stride
arg.summary: Number of bytes in each row of pixel data
